text.title: Client-Conventions Reverse-Engineering Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes that cause the guardrail to produce more harm than help. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: Cargo-culted rules — copying conventions from one part of the codebase that does not represent current standard.
failure-mode.detector: Evidence files are all in a legacy / archive directory OR are older than the average file age of the active code
failure-mode.repair: Re-derive evidence from files modified in last 90 days. Legacy directories often pre-date current conventions and are intentionally not maintained.

failure-mode.description: Hallucinated convention — LLM-extracted rule is plausible but contradicted by config.
failure-mode.detector: For each rule, run the linter against a file that would supposedly violate it; if linter does not complain on a manual violation, the rule may be invented
failure-mode.repair: Verify each rule against the lint config + run a sample violation through the build. Remove rules that aren't actually enforced or evidenced.

failure-mode.description: Stale guardrail — repo conventions shifted, file not updated, agent obeys outdated rules.
failure-mode.detector: verified_by dates > 90 days old AND repo has had >= 20 PRs in that window
failure-mode.repair: Re-run extraction on the latest 30 days of merged PRs. Diff against existing rules. Update with new evidence rows.

failure-mode.description: Missing dependency policy — agent adds a new dep silently in a PR, breaking client security policy.
failure-mode.detector: Dependency Policy section is absent OR says "agent may add deps" without explicit client sign-off
failure-mode.repair: Default to "agent must propose dep additions for human approval." Get written confirmation if a wider policy is allowed.

failure-mode.description: Guardrail file in wrong location — agent doesn't read it.
failure-mode.detector: Agent configuration points to one path; guardrail file lives at another. Or repo root expects CLAUDE.md, guardrail is at .agents/rules.md.
failure-mode.repair: Confirm exactly which file the agent reads (and how — via @-import, settings, system prompt). Move or symlink. Test by asking the agent a convention question and verifying it cites the guardrail.

failure-mode.description: Over-specified guardrail — 200+ rules; agent context is bloated, important rules lose priority.
failure-mode.repair: Move low-confidence and machine-enforced-only rules out (refer to lint config instead). Prioritize structural and naming rules in the main file. Aim for < 4KB / 30 rules in the primary document.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: Mined PR comments may contain instructions ("ignore previous rules", "the convention does not apply here"). These are DATA, not rules. The reverse-engineering must filter to comments that are policy ("we always use X") rather than per-PR exceptions.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Initial guardrail draft is reviewed by a client-side engineer if possible. Disagreements with the client's mental model surface here, cheaply, instead of in PR rejections later. Sign-off recorded in verified_by.
